title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: My favorite! Let's go! I'm going to buy some grapes, so I'll just put them in the carts.
I love Korean melon, so I'm going to buy some today.
This one looks pretty good, so I'm going to put it in my carts.
Let's take a look! I'm going to buy a bag of pre-cooked chicken breasts and some packs of salad. I'm so excited! I found a food that I love!
Sour Crout. Sour Crout is like kimchi, but not spicy. I'm going to buy one.

title: will / shall
examples: My favorite! Let's go! I'm going to buy some grapes, so I'll just put them in the carts.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: Thank you. If you have time before you leave, make sure to check out the food court.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Now I'm in the deli where you can buy different kinds of meats and cheese and all kinds of good stuff.
I couldn't find anything I wanted in the freezer aisle, so let's go to the dry goods.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: I love hearty seated bread like this. It looks like it might be whole wheat bread, but I'm not really sure, but today I'm practicing self-control, so I'm going to put it back on the shelf.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: Thank you. If you have time before you leave, make sure to check out the food court.
